wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer project overview. Explain the application's purpose as a modern MERN stack financial management tool that helps users track loans, calculate stress scores, and simulate financial scenarios. Document the key features including user authentication, loan management, stress analysis, what-if simulation, and interactive dashboards. Describe the target audience and primary use cases. Include the project's architecture philosophy, design principles, and technical approach. Provide context about the financial domain problems this application solves and its position in the personal finance management space.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, npm, Git, MongoDB Atlas), step-by-step installation instructions, environment configuration, and initial setup procedures. Document both manual startup methods and the automated batch script approach. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues including MongoDB connection errors, port conflicts, and CORS problems. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation and explain the expected behavior during initial setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer system. Describe the MERN stack architecture, explaining the separation between frontend (React) and backend (Node.js/Express) components. Document the overall system design, data flow patterns, and component interactions. Explain the MVC pattern implementation, routing architecture, and state management approach using Context API. Include system boundaries, integration points with external services (MongoDB Atlas, Gemini AI), and deployment considerations. Provide architectural diagrams and explain design decisions, trade-offs, and scalability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the User Management System. Detail the complete authentication and authorization flow including JWT-based authentication, user registration, login processes, and session management. Document the User model schema, password hashing with bcryptjs, token generation and validation, and middleware implementation for route protection. Explain the frontend authentication context, protected routes, and user state management. Include security considerations, token expiration handling, and role-based access control. Provide code examples for common authentication scenarios and troubleshooting authentication-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Loan Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Loan Management System. Document the complete CRUD operations for loan management including creation, reading, updating, and deletion of loan records. Explain the EMI calculation algorithms, loan status tracking, and automatic EMI computation based on loan parameters. Detail the Loan model schema, validation rules, and business logic implementation. Cover the frontend components for loan display, table operations, and visualizations. Include loan prioritization logic, status color coding, and user interface patterns. Provide examples of loan data structures, API interactions, and common use cases for loan management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Analysis Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Financial Analysis Engine. Detail the stress score calculation algorithms, debt health assessment methodologies, and financial overview generation. Explain the stress calculator implementation, EMI-to-income ratio calculations, and dynamic stress scoring system. Document the financial overview components, data aggregation patterns, and trend analysis capabilities. Cover the dashboard data structure, chart implementations using Recharts, and real-time data updates. Include the financial calculations utility functions, data validation, and performance optimization strategies. Provide examples of stress score interpretations and financial health indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: What-If Simulation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the What-If Simulation Engine. Explain the scenario definition and simulation framework, including how users can adjust financial parameters to test different outcomes. Document the simulation calculation algorithms, impact analysis methods, and projection modeling techniques. Detail the Simulation model schema, data persistence, and result interpretation mechanisms. Cover the frontend simulation interface, user input handling, and real-time result visualization. Include examples of common simulation scenarios, calculation accuracy, and performance considerations for complex financial projections.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Assistant Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI Assistant Integration. Detail the Gemini AI service integration, conversation flow implementation, and financial recommendation engine. Explain the AI assistant's capabilities including financial insights, fraud detection analysis, and goal planning assistance. Document the frontend AI interface components, voice interaction capabilities, and natural language processing integration. Cover the backend AI service coordination, API proxy patterns, and external service communication. Include examples of AI interactions, response handling, and integration with the Gemini Generative AI platform. Address performance considerations and fallback mechanisms for AI service availability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Emergency Fund Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Emergency Fund Management system. Document the fund setup and configuration processes, transaction processing mechanisms, and savings tracking capabilities. Explain the EmergencyWallet model schema, fund allocation strategies, and withdrawal management procedures. Detail the frontend dashboard components for fund monitoring, transaction history, and savings progress visualization. Cover the automated fund management jobs, notification systems, and user interface patterns. Include examples of fund configuration, transaction workflows, and integration with the broader financial dashboard system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Family Dashboard System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Family Dashboard System. Detail the group creation and management functionality, shared wallet capabilities, and member monitoring features. Explain the FamilyGroup and FamilyMember model schemas, relationship management, and permission systems. Document the shared financial dashboard components, member activity tracking, and collaborative financial planning tools. Cover the frontend family interface, member invitation workflows, and real-time collaboration features. Include examples of family group setup, member management, and shared financial decision-making processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Fraud Detection System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Fraud Detection System. Explain the transaction analysis algorithms, risk assessment methodologies, and alert generation mechanisms. Document the FraudLog model schema, detection criteria, and investigation workflow processes. Detail the frontend fraud analyzer interface, warning modal implementation, and user notification systems. Cover the fraud detection utility functions, pattern recognition algorithms, and real-time monitoring capabilities. Include examples of fraud detection scenarios, false positive handling, and integration with external security services. Address performance considerations for real-time fraud monitoring and data privacy requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Goal Timeline Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Goal Timeline Management system. Detail the goal creation and tracking processes, timeline visualization capabilities, and progress monitoring features. Explain the GoalTimeline model schema, goal categorization, and milestone tracking mechanisms. Document the frontend goal dashboard components, timeline visualization using Recharts, and progress reporting features. Cover the goal timeline utility functions, deadline calculations, and achievement celebration workflows. Include examples of goal setup, timeline management, and integration with the broader financial planning system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer database schema. Detail all entity relationships, field definitions, and data types across the complete model hierarchy. Document primary and foreign key relationships, indexes, and constraints for optimal query performance. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and referential integrity requirements. Include entity relationship diagrams, sample data structures, and data access patterns. Cover data lifecycle management, retention policies, and archival strategies. Address data security considerations, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms. Document data migration paths and version management for schema evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints in the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Include detailed parameter specifications, data validation rules, and error response formats. Cover authentication endpoints (register, login, profile), loan management endpoints, dashboard analytics endpoints, AI assistant endpoints, emergency fund endpoints, family management endpoints, fraud detection endpoints, and goal management endpoints. Provide complete request/response examples, status codes, and common error scenarios. Include rate limiting information, API versioning, and client implementation guidelines. Document the Postman collection usage and testing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React frontend components. Describe the visual appearance, behavior, and user interaction patterns for each major component. Document component props/attributes, events, and customization options. Include usage examples with code snippets and integration patterns. Cover the layout system, navigation components, and responsive design implementation. Detail the chart components using Recharts, interactive data visualization patterns, and state management integration. Explain component composition patterns, styling approaches using Tailwind CSS, and accessibility compliance. Address performance optimization, component lifecycle management, and integration with the Context API for state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for the Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer project. Document code style conventions, ESLint and Prettier configurations, and best practices for both frontend and backend development. Explain the project's development workflow, branch management strategies, and contribution guidelines. Detail testing strategies, debugging approaches, and troubleshooting common development issues. Cover deployment procedures, environment configuration management, and production readiness checks. Include guidelines for extending the application, adding new features, and maintaining code quality. Document the project's TODO items, critical fixes, and ongoing maintenance tasks.

wiki_overview.content:
I'll analyze the Smart Loan Analyzer project systematically, starting with the README and then examining key components to understand the architecture and implementation.

<blog>

# Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

The **Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer** is a modern, full-stack MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React, Node.js) application designed to help users analyze their financial health, calculate loan stress scores, and simulate financial scenarios. Built with a professional dark theme for eye comfort and professional appearance, this application serves as a comprehensive financial management tool.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Financial Tracking**: Monitor and manage multiple loan types with automatic EMI calculations
- **Stress Analysis**: Real-time visualization of financial stress levels based on EMI-to-income ratios
- **Scenario Planning**: Interactive "What-If" simulations for financial decision-making
- **Personalized Recommendations**: AI-powered insights for debt reduction strategies
- **Multi-user Support**: Separate user accounts with role-based access control

### Target Audience

- Individuals seeking to optimize their debt management
- Financial analysts and advisors
- Small business owners managing multiple loans
- Anyone interested in proactive financial planning

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a clean, modular architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[React Frontend]
    B --> C[Express.js Backend]
    C --> D[MongoDB Atlas]
    
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        B1[Authentication Context]
        B2[Dashboard Context]
        B3[Navigation Components]
        B4[Financial Charts]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        C1[REST API Controllers]
        C2[Route Handlers]
        C3[Mongoose Models]
        C4[Utility Functions]
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        D1[User Collections]
        D2[Loan Collections]
        D3[Transaction History]
        D4[Emergency Wallet]
    end
    
    B --> B1
    B --> B2
    B --> B3
    B --> B4
    C --> C1
    C --> C2
    C --> C3
    C --> C4
    C1 --> D1
    C1 --> D2
    C1 --> D3
    C1 --> D4
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

1. **Presentation Layer**: React-based frontend with responsive design
2. **Application Layer**: Express.js backend with RESTful API endpoints
3. **Data Access Layer**: Mongoose ODM with MongoDB Atlas cloud database
4. **Business Logic Layer**: Centralized utility functions for financial calculations

### Design Patterns

- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation of concerns with models, views, and controllers
- **Repository Pattern**: Mongoose models encapsulate data access logic
- **Context Pattern**: React Context API for state management across components
- **Factory Pattern**: Utility functions for financial calculations and AI responses

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**: `backend/server.js`
- Initializes Express.js application with CORS configuration
- Establishes MongoDB connection with retry logic
- Configures comprehensive routing system
- Implements health checks and error handling

**Frontend Entry Point**: `frontend/src/main.jsx`
- React application bootstrap with strict mode
- Root component rendering with provider wrappers
- Development logging for debugging purposes

### Core Modules

**Authentication Controller**: `backend/controllers/authController.js`
- JWT-based authentication with 7-day expiration
- Password hashing using bcryptjs
- User registration and login functionality
- Protected route access control

**Dashboard Controller**: `backend/controllers/dashboardController.js`
- Central financial analysis hub
- Stress score calculation based on EMI ratios
- Loan aggregation and visualization data
- What-If scenario simulation framework

**Financial Calculations**: `backend/utils/finance.js`
- EMI calculation using standard amortization formulas
- Debt health scoring algorithm
- Loan prioritization strategies
- AI assistant response generation

### Configuration Approach

**Backend Configuration**: `backend/package.json`
- Standardized dependencies for production-ready deployment
- Environment variable management with dotenv
- MongoDB Atlas integration
- AI service integrations (Gemini)

**Frontend Configuration**: `frontend/package.json`
- Vite-based build system for fast development
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- React Router for client-side navigation
- Recharts for data visualization

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**:
- `express`: Web application framework
- `mongoose`: MongoDB object modeling
- `bcryptjs`: Password encryption
- `jsonwebtoken`: JWT token generation
- `@google/generative-ai`: AI-powered financial insights

**Frontend Dependencies**:
- `react-router-dom`: Client-side routing
- `recharts`: Interactive data visualization
- `axios`: HTTP client for API communication
- `framer-motion`: Animation library

### Integration Points

**AI Assistant Integration**: `frontend/src/components/AiAssistant.jsx`
- Gemini AI service integration
- Voice input support via Web Speech API
- Real-time conversation interface
- Financial recommendation engine

**MongoDB Atlas Integration**: `backend/server.js`
- Cloud database deployment
- Index optimization for AI features
- Connection pooling and retry logic
- Feature-specific indexing strategy

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[AuthContext] --> B[Protected Routes]
    B --> C[Dashboard Components]
    C --> D[Financial Charts]
    C --> E[Loan Management]
    C --> F[AiAssistant]
    
    G[Finance Utils] --> H[Dashboard Controller]
    H --> I[Loan Controller]
    H --> J[Stress Calculator]
    
    K[User Model] --> L[Loan Model]
    L --> M[Transaction History]
    
    N[API Services] --> O[Backend Routes]
    O --> P[MongoDB Collections]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Authentication System**
- Secure JWT-based authentication
- Role-based access control (user/admin)
- Session persistence with automatic token refresh
- Password encryption with bcryptjs

**Loan Management System**
- Comprehensive loan tracking with multiple types
- Automatic EMI calculation using standard formulas
- Status management (ACTIVE, PAID, PENDING)
- Historical loan data retention

**Financial Stress Analysis**
- Real-time stress score calculation
- EMI-to-income ratio monitoring
- Visual stress gauge with color coding
- Trend analysis over time periods

**What-If Scenario Simulation**
- Interactive financial scenario testing
- Impact analysis on stress scores
- Savings projection calculations
- Debt-free date predictions

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced AI Integration**: `frontend/src/services/geminiService.js`
- Natural language processing for financial queries
- Context-aware recommendation system
- Voice-enabled interaction capabilities
- Personalized financial advice engine

**Real-time Data Visualization**: `frontend/src/components/FinancialOverview.jsx`
- Interactive charts using Recharts library
- Responsive design for all device sizes
- Dark theme with gradient accents
- Animated transitions and smooth interactions

**Comprehensive State Management**: `frontend/src/context/DashboardContext.jsx`
- Centralized financial data management
- Real-time updates across components
- Context-based data sharing
- Performance optimization through selective re-renders

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Register
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Load Data
    Dashboard --> LoanManagement: Manage Loans
    Dashboard --> StressAnalysis: Analyze Stress
    Dashboard --> WhatIfSimulation: Test Scenarios
    Dashboard --> AiAssistant: Get Advice
    LoanManagement --> Dashboard: Back to Dashboard
    StressAnalysis --> Dashboard: Back to Dashboard
    WhatIfSimulation --> Dashboard: Back to Dashboard
    AiAssistant --> Dashboard: Back to Dashboard
    Authenticated --> Unauthenticated: Logout
```

The Smart Loan & Debt Stress Analyzer represents a sophisticated financial management solution that combines modern web technologies with practical financial analysis tools. Its modular architecture ensures maintainability and extensibility, while the comprehensive feature set addresses real-world financial planning needs.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [server.js](backend/server.js)
- [main.jsx](frontend/src/main.jsx)
- [App.jsx](frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [User.js](backend/models/User.js)
- [Loan.js](backend/models/Loan.js)
- [authController.js](backend/controllers/authController.js)
- [dashboardController.js](backend/controllers/dashboardController.js)
- [api.js](frontend/src/services/api.js)
- [finance.js](backend/utils/finance.js)
- [Layout.jsx](frontend/src/components/Layout.jsx)
- [AuthContext.jsx](frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx)
- [package.json](backend/package.json)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [AiAssistant.jsx](frontend/src/components/AiAssistant.jsx)

</blog>
